---
title: "When One Test Is Not Enough"
subtitle: "Multiple hypothesis testing, demystified."
date: "2026-04-24"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-tools: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

<!-- TODO: cover image assets/multiple-testing-cover.jpg (generate with Gemini) -->

```{=html}
<script>
(function loadGSAP() {
  var savedDefine = window.define;
  window.define = undefined;
  var s1 = document.createElement('script');
  s1.src = 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/gsap/3.12.5/gsap.min.js';
  s1.onload = function() {
    window.define = savedDefine;
    document.dispatchEvent(new Event('gsap-ready'));
  };
  s1.onerror = function() {
    window.define = savedDefine;
    document.dispatchEvent(new Event('gsap-ready'));
  };
  document.head.appendChild(s1);
})();
</script>

<div class="gs-theater" id="gs-theater">
  <div class="gs-screen" id="gs-screen">
    <div style="text-align:center;padding:40px;color:#5a5a5a;font-style:italic;">Loading interactive walkthrough...</div>
  </div>
  <div class="gs-timeline-bar">
    <div class="gs-timeline-fill" id="gs-tl-fill"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="gs-nav-controls">
    <button class="gs-nav-btn gs-nav-prev" id="gs-prev" disabled>Previous</button>
    <span class="gs-nav-counter" id="gs-counter">1 / 9</span>
    <button class="gs-nav-btn gs-nav-next" id="gs-next">Next</button>
  </div>
  <div class="gs-nav-restart"><button class="gs-restart-btn" id="gs-restart">Restart</button></div>
</div>

<script>
function initTheater() {
  var hasGsap = typeof gsap !== 'undefined';

  var screen = document.getElementById('gs-screen');
  var tlFill = document.getElementById('gs-tl-fill');
  var prevBtn = document.getElementById('gs-prev');
  var nextBtn = document.getElementById('gs-next');
  var counterEl = document.getElementById('gs-counter');
  var restartBtn = document.getElementById('gs-restart');
  var currentScene = 0;

  function clearScreen() { if (hasGsap) gsap.killTweensOf(screen.querySelectorAll('*')); screen.innerHTML = ''; screen.style.opacity = 1; }
  function aFromTo(t, f, to) { if (hasGsap) gsap.fromTo(t, f, to); }
  function aTo(t, to) { if (hasGsap) gsap.to(t, to); }

  function showCharacter(src, text) {
    var wrap = document.createElement('div');
    wrap.className = 'gs-char-scene';
    wrap.innerHTML = '<div class="gs-char-big"><img src="' + src + '" alt="Guide" /></div>' +
      '<div class="gs-bubble"><div class="gs-bubble-tail"></div><p>' + text + '</p></div>';
    screen.appendChild(wrap);
    if (hasGsap) {
      aFromTo(wrap.querySelector('.gs-char-big'),
        { xPercent: -30, opacity: 0 },
        { xPercent: 0, opacity: 1, duration: 0.4, ease: 'power2.out',
          onComplete: function() {
            gsap.set(wrap.querySelector('.gs-char-big'), { clearProps: 'all' });
          }
        }
      );
      aFromTo(wrap.querySelector('.gs-bubble'),
        { opacity: 0 },
        { opacity: 1, duration: 0.4, ease: 'power2.out', delay: 0.3 }
      );
      aTo(wrap.querySelector('.gs-char-big img'), {
        y: -6, duration: 2, ease: 'sine.inOut', repeat: -1, yoyo: true, delay: 0.4
      });
    }
  }

  /* Deterministic "random" indices for false positives (seed-like consistency) */
  var falsePositiveCells = [7, 23, 41, 68, 92];

  function makeGrid(cellStates, animate) {
    var html = '<div class="mt-grid">';
    for (var i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
      var cls = 'mt-cell';
      if (cellStates && cellStates[i]) cls += ' ' + cellStates[i];
      html += '<div class="' + cls + '" data-idx="' + i + '"></div>';
    }
    html += '</div>';
    return html;
  }

  var scenes = [
    /* Scene 1: Intro with persona-guide */
    function() {
      showCharacter('../../assets/persona-guide.svg', 'Five percent of your discoveries are guaranteed to be wrong. Let me show you why, and how we fix it.');
    },

    /* Scene 2: Single coin with persona-analyst */
    function() {
      showCharacter('../../assets/persona-analyst.svg', 'Start simple. One coin, 100 flips, 47 heads. Is it fair? Probably yes. The math says we cannot rule out a fair coin, so we move on.');
    },

    /* Scene 3: 100 tests grid, ~5 false positives */
    function() {
      var states = {};
      screen.innerHTML = '<div class="gs-act-wrap">' +
        '<div class="gs-act-tag">The Trap</div>' +
        '<div class="gs-act-name">Now run 100 tests at once.</div>' +
        '<div style="text-align:center;color:rgba(44,62,80,0.6);font-size:0.95rem;margin-bottom:1.25rem;">All 100 coins are fair. Watch what happens anyway.</div>' +
        makeGrid(null, false) +
        '<div class="mt-caption" id="mt-cap3" style="opacity:0;">About 5 of these tests look "significant." None of these coins are biased. We just ran enough tests.</div>' +
        '</div>';
      if (hasGsap) {
        falsePositiveCells.forEach(function(idx, i) {
          var cell = screen.querySelector('.mt-cell[data-idx="' + idx + '"]');
          if (cell) {
            gsap.to(cell, {
              backgroundColor: '#c27c5a',
              borderColor: '#c27c5a',
              duration: 0.3,
              delay: 0.6 + i * 0.15,
              ease: 'power2.out'
            });
          }
        });
        aTo('#mt-cap3', { opacity: 1, duration: 0.5, delay: 1.6 });
      } else {
        falsePositiveCells.forEach(function(idx) {
          var cell = screen.querySelector('.mt-cell[data-idx="' + idx + '"]');
          if (cell) { cell.style.backgroundColor = '#c27c5a'; cell.style.borderColor = '#c27c5a'; }
        });
        var cap = document.getElementById('mt-cap3');
        if (cap) cap.style.opacity = 1;
      }
    },

    /* Scene 4: Line chart (rendered from Python, embedded as image) */
    function() {
      var imgEl = document.getElementById('mt-chart-falsepos');
      var src = imgEl ? imgEl.src : '';
      screen.innerHTML = '<div class="gs-act-wrap">' +
        '<div class="gs-act-tag">The Math</div>' +
        '<div class="gs-act-name">The math of the trap</div>' +
        '<div style="text-align:center;"><img src="' + src + '" alt="False positive probability curve" style="max-width:100%;max-height:380px;border-radius:6px;" /></div>' +
        '<div class="mt-caption">Run 100 fair tests, you are 99 percent likely to call at least one biased.</div>' +
        '</div>';
      if (hasGsap) {
        aFromTo(screen.querySelector('img'), { opacity: 0, y: 20 }, { opacity: 1, y: 0, duration: 0.5, ease: 'power2.out' });
      }
    },

    /* Scene 5: Bonferroni grid */
    function() {
      screen.innerHTML = '<div class="gs-act-wrap">' +
        '<div class="gs-act-tag">Correction 1</div>' +
        '<div class="gs-act-name">Bonferroni: lower the bar</div>' +
        '<div class="mt-threshold-wrap">' +
          '<div class="mt-threshold-labels"><span>p &lt; 0.05</span><span id="mt-thresh-new" style="opacity:0;">p &lt; 0.0005</span></div>' +
          '<div class="mt-threshold-line" id="mt-thresh-line"></div>' +
        '</div>' +
        makeGrid(null, false) +
        '<div class="mt-caption" id="mt-cap5" style="opacity:0;">Strict but safe. We just made it almost impossible to call anything significant by accident.</div>' +
        '</div>';

      falsePositiveCells.forEach(function(idx) {
        var cell = screen.querySelector('.mt-cell[data-idx="' + idx + '"]');
        if (cell) { cell.style.backgroundColor = '#c27c5a'; cell.style.borderColor = '#c27c5a'; }
      });

      if (hasGsap) {
        gsap.to('#mt-thresh-line', { width: '100%', duration: 0.6, delay: 0.4, ease: 'power2.out' });
        gsap.to('#mt-thresh-new', { opacity: 1, duration: 0.3, delay: 0.8 });
        falsePositiveCells.forEach(function(idx, i) {
          var cell = screen.querySelector('.mt-cell[data-idx="' + idx + '"]');
          if (cell) {
            gsap.to(cell, {
              backgroundColor: '#faf6ef',
              borderColor: 'rgba(44,62,80,0.2)',
              duration: 0.4,
              delay: 1.2 + i * 0.1,
              ease: 'power2.out'
            });
          }
        });
        aTo('#mt-cap5', { opacity: 1, duration: 0.5, delay: 2.0 });
      } else {
        var cap = document.getElementById('mt-cap5');
        if (cap) cap.style.opacity = 1;
        falsePositiveCells.forEach(function(idx) {
          var cell = screen.querySelector('.mt-cell[data-idx="' + idx + '"]');
          if (cell) { cell.style.backgroundColor = '#faf6ef'; cell.style.borderColor = 'rgba(44,62,80,0.2)'; }
        });
      }
    },

    /* Scene 6: Real effects + Bonferroni misses */
    function() {
      var trueEffects = [0,1,2,3,10,11,12,13,20,21,22,23,30,31,32,33,40,41,42,43];
      var detectedBonf = [0, 11, 22];
      var falsePosBonf = [];
      var states = {};
      for (var i = 0; i < 100; i++) states[i] = '';

      screen.innerHTML = '<div class="gs-act-wrap">' +
        '<div class="gs-act-tag">The Cost</div>' +
        '<div class="gs-act-name">But what if some effects are real?</div>' +
        '<div style="text-align:center;color:rgba(44,62,80,0.6);font-size:0.95rem;margin-bottom:1rem;">100 tests. 20 are TRUE effects (real biased coins). 80 are nulls.</div>' +
        '<div class="mt-grid" id="mt-grid6"></div>' +
        '<div class="mt-legend">' +
          '<span class="mt-legend-item"><span class="mt-swatch mt-swatch-detected"></span> True effect detected</span>' +
          '<span class="mt-legend-item"><span class="mt-swatch mt-swatch-fp"></span> False positive</span>' +
          '<span class="mt-legend-item"><span class="mt-swatch mt-swatch-missed"></span> No detection</span>' +
        '</div>' +
        '<div class="mt-caption" id="mt-cap6" style="opacity:0;">Bonferroni controlled errors. We also missed most of the real effects.</div>' +
        '</div>';

      var grid = document.getElementById('mt-grid6');
      for (var i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
        var div = document.createElement('div');
        div.className = 'mt-cell';
        div.setAttribute('data-idx', i);
        if (trueEffects.indexOf(i) !== -1) {
          div.style.border = '1px solid rgba(44,62,80,0.3)';
        }
        grid.appendChild(div);
      }

      if (hasGsap) {
        trueEffects.forEach(function(idx, i) {
          var cell = grid.querySelector('.mt-cell[data-idx="' + idx + '"]');
          if (cell) {
            gsap.to(cell, { backgroundColor: 'rgba(44,62,80,0.15)', duration: 0.2, delay: 0.3 + i * 0.03 });
          }
        });
        gsap.delayedCall(1.2, function() {
          trueEffects.forEach(function(idx) {
            var cell = grid.querySelector('.mt-cell[data-idx="' + idx + '"]');
            if (cell && detectedBonf.indexOf(idx) === -1) {
              gsap.to(cell, { backgroundColor: '#faf6ef', duration: 0.3 });
            }
          });
          detectedBonf.forEach(function(idx) {
            var cell = grid.querySelector('.mt-cell[data-idx="' + idx + '"]');
            if (cell) {
              gsap.to(cell, { backgroundColor: '#2c3e50', borderColor: '#2c3e50', duration: 0.3 });
            }
          });
        });
        aTo('#mt-cap6', { opacity: 1, duration: 0.5, delay: 2.2 });
      } else {
        detectedBonf.forEach(function(idx) {
          var cell = grid.querySelector('.mt-cell[data-idx="' + idx + '"]');
          if (cell) { cell.style.backgroundColor = '#2c3e50'; cell.style.borderColor = '#2c3e50'; }
        });
        var cap = document.getElementById('mt-cap6');
        if (cap) cap.style.opacity = 1;
      }
    },

    /* Scene 7: BH scatter (rendered from Python, embedded as image) */
    function() {
      var imgEl = document.getElementById('mt-chart-bh');
      var src = imgEl ? imgEl.src : '';
      screen.innerHTML = '<div class="gs-act-wrap">' +
        '<div class="gs-act-tag">Correction 2</div>' +
        '<div class="gs-act-name">Benjamini-Hochberg: a smarter line</div>' +
        '<div style="text-align:center;"><img src="' + src + '" alt="BH procedure scatter plot" style="max-width:100%;max-height:380px;border-radius:6px;" /></div>' +
        '<div class="mt-caption">Sort the p-values. Find the last one that beats its rising threshold. Reject everything up to that point.</div>' +
        '</div>';
      if (hasGsap) {
        aFromTo(screen.querySelector('img'), { opacity: 0, y: 20 }, { opacity: 1, y: 0, duration: 0.5, ease: 'power2.out' });
      }
    },

    /* Scene 8: Side by side comparison */
    function() {
      function miniGrid(label, detected, fps, totalFlagged, totalFP) {
        var trueEffects = [0,1,2,3,10,11,12,13,20,21,22,23,30,31,32,33,40,41,42,43];
        var html = '<div class="mt-mini-panel"><div class="mt-mini-label">' + label + '</div><div class="mt-mini-grid">';
        for (var i = 0; i < 100; i++) {
          var cls = 'mt-mini-cell';
          if (detected.indexOf(i) !== -1) cls += ' mt-mini-detected';
          else if (fps.indexOf(i) !== -1) cls += ' mt-mini-fp';
          html += '<div class="' + cls + '"></div>';
        }
        html += '</div><div class="mt-mini-stats">' + totalFlagged + ' flagged, ' + totalFP + ' false</div></div>';
        return html;
      }

      var trueEffects = [0,1,2,3,10,11,12,13,20,21,22,23,30,31,32,33,40,41,42,43];

      var uncDetected = [0,1,2,3,10,11,12,13,20,21,22,23,30,31,32,33,40,41,42,43];
      var uncFP = [7,23,34,52,58,67,71,85,92];
      uncDetected = uncDetected.filter(function(x) { return uncFP.indexOf(x) === -1; });

      var bonfDetected = [0, 11, 22];
      var bonfFP = [];

      var bhDetected = [0,1,2,3,10,11,12,13,20,21,22,23,30,31,40,41,42];
      var bhFP = [52, 85];

      screen.innerHTML = '<div class="gs-act-wrap">' +
        '<div class="gs-act-tag">Comparison</div>' +
        '<div class="gs-act-name">Side by side</div>' +
        '<div class="mt-comparison">' +
          miniGrid('Uncorrected', uncDetected, uncFP, '47 flagged', '9 false') +
          miniGrid('Bonferroni', bonfDetected, bonfFP, '3 flagged', '0 false') +
          miniGrid('BH', bhDetected, bhFP, '19 flagged', '2 false') +
        '</div>' +
        '<div class="mt-caption">BH catches more truth at the cost of a small, controlled rate of false alarms.</div>' +
        '</div>';
      if (hasGsap) {
        aFromTo('.mt-mini-panel', { opacity: 0, y: 20 }, { opacity: 1, y: 0, duration: 0.4, stagger: 0.2, ease: 'power2.out' });
      }
    },

    /* Scene 9: Conclusion with persona-analyst */
    function() {
      showCharacter('../../assets/persona-analyst.svg', 'Bonferroni protects against any false alarm. Benjamini-Hochberg accepts a controlled rate of false alarms in exchange for finding more truth. Pick the mistake you can live with.');
    }
  ];

  function goTo(n) {
    if (n < 0 || n >= scenes.length) return;
    currentScene = n;
    clearScreen();
    scenes[n]();
    tlFill.style.width = ((n + 1) / scenes.length * 100) + '%';
    counterEl.textContent = (n + 1) + ' / ' + scenes.length;
    prevBtn.disabled = (n === 0);
    nextBtn.disabled = (n === scenes.length - 1);
  }

  prevBtn.addEventListener('click', function() { goTo(currentScene - 1); });
  nextBtn.addEventListener('click', function() { goTo(currentScene + 1); });
  restartBtn.addEventListener('click', function() { goTo(0); });

  document.addEventListener('keydown', function(e) {
    var rect = document.getElementById('gs-theater').getBoundingClientRect();
    if (rect.top > window.innerHeight || rect.bottom < 0) return;
    if (e.key === 'ArrowRight') { goTo(currentScene + 1); e.preventDefault(); }
    if (e.key === 'ArrowLeft') { goTo(currentScene - 1); e.preventDefault(); }
  });

  goTo(0);
}
if (typeof gsap !== 'undefined') { initTheater(); }
else { document.addEventListener('gsap-ready', initTheater); }
</script>
```

## The Multiple Testing Problem

When you run a single statistical test at the 5% significance level, there is a 5% chance of a false positive. That sounds manageable. But what happens when you run many tests at once?

```{python}
#| label: fig-false-positive-curve
#| fig-cap: "Probability of at least one false positive as the number of tests increases"
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib

matplotlib.rcParams['font.family'] = 'serif'
matplotlib.rcParams['axes.facecolor'] = '#faf6ef'
matplotlib.rcParams['figure.facecolor'] = '#faf6ef'
matplotlib.rcParams['axes.edgecolor'] = '#2c3e50'
matplotlib.rcParams['axes.labelcolor'] = '#2c3e50'
matplotlib.rcParams['xtick.color'] = '#2c3e50'
matplotlib.rcParams['ytick.color'] = '#2c3e50'

m = np.arange(1, 101)
prob = 1 - (1 - 0.05)**m

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
ax.plot(m, prob * 100, color='#2c3e50', linewidth=2.5)
ax.fill_between(m, 0, prob * 100, color='#2c3e50', alpha=0.06)

ax.axhline(y=5, color='#b8945a', linestyle='--', linewidth=1, alpha=0.6)
ax.annotate('m = 20: ~64%', xy=(20, 64.2), fontsize=10, color='#b8945a', fontweight='bold',
            xytext=(30, 50), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#b8945a', lw=1.2))
ax.annotate('m = 100: ~99.4%', xy=(100, 99.4), fontsize=10, color='#c27c5a', fontweight='bold',
            xytext=(70, 85), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#c27c5a', lw=1.2))

ax.set_xlabel('Number of tests (m)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('P(at least one false positive) %', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('The Multiple Testing Trap', fontsize=14, color='#2c3e50', fontweight='bold')
ax.set_ylim(0, 105)
ax.set_xlim(1, 100)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.1, color='#2c3e50')
ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)

fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{=html}
<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  var imgs = document.querySelectorAll('#fig-false-positive-curve img, [id*="fig-false-positive-curve"] img');
  if (imgs.length > 0) {
    var hidden = document.createElement('img');
    hidden.id = 'mt-chart-falsepos';
    hidden.src = imgs[0].src;
    hidden.style.display = 'none';
    document.body.appendChild(hidden);
  }
});
</script>
```

The curve above shows how quickly certainty collapses. At 20 tests, you have a 64% chance of at least one false positive. At 100 tests, it is nearly guaranteed.

## Correction Methods

### Bonferroni

The simplest fix: divide the significance threshold by the number of tests. If you run 100 tests, each test must clear p < 0.0005 instead of p < 0.05. This controls the **family-wise error rate (FWER)**, the probability that any single test is a false positive. It is strict, and it works. But it can be too conservative, especially when many of the tests are testing real effects.

### Benjamini-Hochberg (BH)

A more modern approach that controls the **false discovery rate (FDR)**, the expected proportion of rejected hypotheses that are false positives. Instead of a fixed threshold, it uses a rising line.

```{python}
#| label: fig-bh-procedure
#| fig-cap: "The Benjamini-Hochberg procedure: sorted p-values vs. the BH threshold line"
np.random.seed(42)

n_tests = 100
n_true = 20

p_null = np.random.uniform(0, 1, n_tests - n_true)
p_true = np.random.beta(1, 20, n_true)

p_values = np.concatenate([p_true, p_null])
is_true = np.array([True]*n_true + [False]*(n_tests - n_true))

sorted_idx = np.argsort(p_values)
p_sorted = p_values[sorted_idx]
is_true_sorted = is_true[sorted_idx]

ranks = np.arange(1, n_tests + 1)
bh_threshold = ranks * 0.05 / n_tests

bh_cutoff = 0
for k in range(n_tests):
    if p_sorted[k] <= bh_threshold[k]:
        bh_cutoff = k + 1

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))

colors = ['#2c3e50' if t else '#b8945a' for t in is_true_sorted]
for i in range(n_tests):
    marker = 'o' if is_true_sorted[i] else 's'
    ax.scatter(ranks[i], p_sorted[i], c=colors[i], s=30, marker=marker, zorder=3, alpha=0.8)

ax.plot(ranks, bh_threshold, color='#b8945a', linewidth=2, linestyle='--', label='BH threshold (q=0.05)')

if bh_cutoff > 0:
    ax.axvline(x=bh_cutoff, color='#c27c5a', linewidth=1.5, linestyle=':', alpha=0.7)
    ax.annotate(f'BH cutoff at rank {bh_cutoff}', xy=(bh_cutoff, p_sorted[bh_cutoff-1]),
                fontsize=10, color='#c27c5a', fontweight='bold',
                xytext=(bh_cutoff + 10, p_sorted[bh_cutoff-1] + 0.15),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#c27c5a', lw=1.2))

ax.fill_between(ranks[:bh_cutoff], 0, p_sorted[:bh_cutoff], color='#2c3e50', alpha=0.08)

ax.scatter([], [], c='#2c3e50', s=30, marker='o', label='True effect')
ax.scatter([], [], c='#b8945a', s=30, marker='s', label='Null (no effect)')

ax.set_xlabel('Rank (sorted p-values)', fontsize=12)
ax.set_ylabel('p-value', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Benjamini-Hochberg Procedure', fontsize=14, color='#2c3e50', fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10, framealpha=0.9)
ax.set_xlim(0, 105)
ax.set_ylim(-0.02, 1.05)
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.1, color='#2c3e50')
ax.spines['top'].set_visible(False)
ax.spines['right'].set_visible(False)

fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{=html}
<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  var imgs = document.querySelectorAll('#fig-bh-procedure img, [id*="fig-bh-procedure"] img');
  if (imgs.length > 0) {
    var hidden = document.createElement('img');
    hidden.id = 'mt-chart-bh';
    hidden.src = imgs[0].src;
    hidden.style.display = 'none';
    document.body.appendChild(hidden);
  }
});
</script>
```

The BH procedure sorts all p-values from smallest to largest, then draws a line from (1, 0.05/m) to (m, 0.05). Any p-value that falls below this line is rejected. The key insight: it finds the largest rank k where p(k) is still below the threshold, then rejects everything from rank 1 through k.

## When to Use Which

**Use Bonferroni when:**

- The cost of a single false positive is catastrophic (clinical trials, genomics with functional follow-up)
- You are running a small number of planned comparisons
- You need to guarantee FWER control

**Use Benjamini-Hochberg when:**

- You are screening many hypotheses and expect some real effects
- You can tolerate a controlled proportion of false discoveries
- Power matters more than absolute certainty (exploratory analysis, feature selection)

## Key Takeaways

1. Running multiple tests inflates false positives exponentially
2. Bonferroni divides the threshold by the number of tests (strict, controls FWER)
3. Benjamini-Hochberg uses a rising threshold line (flexible, controls FDR)
4. Neither is universally better. The right choice depends on the cost of errors in your specific context
